install_patterns: npm scripts: `npm run dev` (concurrently runs mock + start)
Implicit `npm install` to resolve package.json dependencies
Browser-side remote script loading via <script src> from third-party CDNs (cdn.tailwindcss.com, cdn.jsdelivr.net, unpkg.com)
risk_notes: All npm dependencies use caret (^) ranges — not exact-pinned; transitive/minor updates can drift at install time.
No lockfile (package-lock.json / npm-shrinkwrap) is shown, so reproducible installs are not guaranteed.
Frontend loads Tailwind, Chart.js, and Lucide from third-party CDNs without Subresource Integrity (SRI) hashes — supply-chain tampering or CDN compromise could inject arbitrary JS into the dashboard.
Lucide is pinned to the moving tag `@latest` (unpkg.com/lucide@latest), giving silent auto-upgrades to whatever version the registry serves.
Tailwind CDN (cdn.tailwindcss.com) is a runtime JIT loader intended for prototyping — version is entirely controlled by the CDN with no version pinning.
External Google Fonts @import leaks client IPs/User-Agents and represents an additional uncontrolled third-party dependency.

CORS middleware is enabled with default permissive configuration (`app.use(cors())`) on both the trading backend and mock brokerage server — any origin can hit trading APIs including `/api/trade`, `/api/toggle-trading`, and `/api/clear-history`.
Trading and admin endpoints (`/api/trade`, `/api/toggle-trading`, `/api/clear-history`, `/trigger-drop`, `/reset-price`) have no authentication, authorization, rate limiting, or CSRF protection.

Production WebSocket URL `wss://api.brokerage.com/live` is hardcoded with automatic silent fallback to `ws://localhost:8081/live` (unencrypted) — a connectivity failure to the real broker causes the engine to keep running against an unauthenticated mock without operator confirmation.
No TLS/certificate verification options or auth tokens are configured on the outbound WebSocket client to the brokerage.
SQLite database file path is local and unencrypted; no credentials or secrets management is shown but also none enforced for a system that purports to execute live trades.
`sqlite3` package historically relies on native bindings and prebuilt binary downloads at install time — unpinned version increases native-build supply-chain exposure.

findings.evidence: Lucide is pinned to the moving tag `@latest` (unpkg.com/lucide@latest), giving silent auto-upgrades to whatever version the registry serves.

supply_chain_meaning: Static manifest/install-pattern scan; LLM dependency extraction from agent output; OSV lookup attempted (no matching advisories or no pinned versions).
